items.title: FLSA Definitions
items.description: Section 3 of the Fair Labor Standards Act — defines employee, employer, enterprise, employ, and other key terms.

items.title: Minimum Wage
items.description: Section 6 of the FLSA — establishes the federal minimum wage; subsection (d) is the Equal Pay Act.

items.title: Maximum Hours and Overtime
items.description: Section 7 of the FLSA — requires overtime pay at 1.5x the regular rate over 40 hours; includes the 7(i) retail/commission exemption.

items.title: Recordkeeping and Investigations
items.description: Section 11 of the FLSA — authority to investigate and the employer duty to keep records.

items.title: Child Labor Provisions
items.description: Section 12 of the FLSA — prohibits oppressive child labor and shipment of goods produced with it.

items.title: Subminimum Wage for Workers with Disabilities
items.description: Section 14(c) of the FLSA — allows special minimum wages, under certificate, for workers whose disabilities affect productivity.

items.title: Prohibited Acts
items.description: Section 15 of the FLSA — bars retaliation, recordkeeping violations, and shipment of 'hot goods'.

items.title: Penalties and Employer Liability
items.description: Section 16 of the FLSA — back wages, liquidated damages, civil money penalties, and private right of action.

items.title: Relation to Other Laws
items.description: Section 18 of the FLSA — state and local laws with higher standards are not preempted.

items.title: Records To Be Kept by Employers
items.description: Recordkeeping requirements under the FLSA — what payroll and time records employers must maintain.

items.title: Wage Payments Under the FLSA
items.description: Tip credit for tipped employees, and the treatment of board, lodging, and other facilities and deductions.

items.title: Overtime Exemptions for White-Collar Employees
items.description: Executive, administrative, professional, computer, and outside sales exemptions — including the salary-basis and duties tests.

items.title: Domestic Service Employment
items.description: Application of the FLSA to domestic service workers, including home care and the companionship exemption.

items.title: FLSA Application to Public Agencies
items.description: Special FLSA rules for state and local government employees, including compensatory time and fire/police partial exemptions.

items.title: Child Labor Regulations
items.description: Employment of minors — hours and occupation standards for 14- and 15-year-olds (Reg 3) and the Hazardous Occupations Orders (HOs).

items.title: Child Labor Civil Money Penalties
items.description: Assessment of civil money penalties for child labor violations under the FLSA.

items.title: Overtime Compensation and the Regular Rate
items.description: How to compute the regular rate of pay and overtime, including bonuses, commissions, and other payments.

items.title: Hours Worked
items.description: What counts as compensable hours worked — waiting time, on-call time, breaks, training, and travel time.

items.title: McNamara-O'Hara Service Contract Act
items.description: Prevailing wage and fringe benefit requirements for service employees on federal service contracts.

items.title: Davis-Bacon and Related Acts
items.description: Prevailing wage requirements for laborers and mechanics on federally funded construction contracts.

items.title: Migrant and Seasonal Agricultural Worker Protection Act
items.description: MSPA regulations — registration of farm labor contractors and protections for migrant and seasonal farmworkers.

items.title: H-2A Enforcement
items.description: Enforcement of contractual obligations for temporary agricultural workers admitted under the H-2A visa program.

items.title: H-2B Enforcement
items.description: Enforcement of obligations for temporary non-agricultural workers admitted under the H-2B visa program.

items.title: Employment of Workers with Disabilities at Subminimum Wages
items.description: Section 14(c) certificate program rules for paying special minimum wages to workers with disabilities.

items.title: The Family and Medical Leave Act
items.description: FMLA regulations — up to 12 weeks of job-protected leave for qualifying family and medical reasons.

items.title: Family and Medical Leave Act (Statute)
items.description: The FMLA statute — findings and purposes for job-protected family and medical leave.

items.title: Migrant and Seasonal Agricultural Worker Protection Act (Statute)
items.description: The MSPA statute — protections, disclosures, and farm labor contractor registration for farmworkers.
